metadata.title: Translate the Hidden Curriculum with Claude: What College Assumes You Already Know

beats.narration_text: The institution never explains its own norms. Students who don't know them pay a hidden attention tax from week one.

The hidden curriculum is the set of unwritten rules that govern college success — and it is never taught because doing so is expensive and assumes adults figure it out. Five terms mislead most incoming students: Syllabus, Office Hours, Plagiarism, Final Exam, Faculty. Each suggests something familiar; each means something different in the college context.
Ask Claude to identify hidden curriculum signals and generate the translation table.

props.command: claude "I'm going to paste a college syllabus passage. Identify every hidden curriculum signal — anything communicated implicitly, not explicitly — and explain what behavior each calls for. Then generate a translation table with 5 rows: Syllabus, Office Hours, Plagiarism, Final Exam, Faculty. Columns: Term | What it suggests | What it means in college | Costly first-semester mistake | What to do instead." [syllabus passage from Chapter 1]

beats.narration_text: The translation table: Office Hours entry reads professor available by appointment — means professor is waiting for you and almost no one comes.

props.code:
| Term        | What it suggests         | What it means in college          |
|-------------|--------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Syllabus    | course schedule          | contract + grading rules          |
| Office Hours| professor available      | professor waiting; almost no-shows|
| Plagiarism  | intentional cheating     | includes sloppy attribution       |
| Final Exam  | exam at end of semester  | 25-40% of final grade in 1 sitting|
| Faculty     | teacher                  | researcher with grading authority |

# Costly mistakes / What to do instead
# Office Hours: never going → go once in week 2
# Final Exam: treat like any exam → prepare 2+ weeks ahead

beats.narration_text: Table generated: 5 rows, all sections populated. Office Hours: professor available means professor is actively waiting; most seats empty. Final Exam: exam at end of semester means 25 to 40 percent of your final grade in one sitting — the largest single grade event of the term. Plagiarism: suggests intentional cheating — includes sloppy note-taking that loses source attribution. Each what-to-do-instead entry is behavioral and specific.
Apply the same analysis to the viewer's actual course syllabus.

props.command: claude "Apply hidden curriculum analysis to my actual course syllabus. Identify signals beyond the standard 5 — include attendance policy, participation grading, late-work language. For each: what it says vs. what it means, and one behavioral action." [paste your syllabus]

beats.narration_text: Live syllabus analysis: Claude identifies 3 additional hidden signals not in the standard 5 — attendance is expected means 3 unexcused absences drops a letter grade, policy in footnote 4 of the actual syllabus. Participation counts 15 percent means cold-calling is possible and prepared students are not all equal. Late work policy: case by case means ask before the deadline, not after. The hidden signals in a real syllabus are always more specific than the standard 5.

The hidden curriculum sorts outcomes silently. Students who arrive already fluent pay a lower attention tax. The translation table does not change the institution — it equalizes access to the operating manual. The five terms are the highest-leverage translations; a real syllabus will always contain more.
Next: run the Five Whys to find the motivation loud enough to power 1 AM.
Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
